result.stdout:
ErikEJ.TSQLAnalyzerCli 1.0.40+8b0c2a409bccbf5e64fc99657c53b00ea01f97c0
Copyright © ErikEJ 2025 - https://github.com/ErikEJ/SqlServer.Rules

  -i, --input               .sql script file(s) to analyze - if not supplied,
                            assumes all .sql files under current directory.

  -c, --connectionstring    Connection string of the database to analyze

  -r, --rules               Rules to ignore:
                            'Rules:-SqlServer.Rules.SRD0010;-Smells.*'

  -s, --sqlversion          SQL Server release version, like 'Sql160' or
                            'SqlAzure' - defaults to 'Sql160'

  -n, --nologo              Compact output without logo and informational
                            messages

  -f, --format              PREVIEW: Attempt to format the .sql files selected
                            for analysis.

  -o, --output              Optional file name of output file in .xml or .json
                            format

  -a, --analyzers           Directory path of additional analyzer .dll files,
                            can be specified multiple times.

  -t, --text                String to analyze

  --help                    Display this help screen.

  --version                 Display version information.


SAMPLES:

# Analyze all scripts in current folder and sub-folders
tsqlanalyze

## Analyze a single script
tsqlanalyze -i C:\scripts\sproc.sql

## Analyze scripts in a folder
tsqlanalyze -i "c:\database scripts"

## Analyze scripts in folders with a wildcard path and a full folder path
tsqlanalyze -i c:\database_scripts\sp_*.sql "c:\old scripts"

## Analyze a script with a rule filter
tsqlanalyze -i C:\scripts\sproc.sql -r Rules:-SqlServer.Rules.SRD0004

## Analyze a script for a specific SQL Server version
tsqlanalyze -i C:\scripts\sproc.sql -s SqlAzure

## Analyze a .dacpac and save results to an xml file
tsqlanalyze -i C:\scripts\Chinook.dacpac -o result.xml

## Analyze a single file, and reformat (PREVIEW)
tsqlanalyze -i C:\scripts\sproc.sql -f

## Analyze a .zip file with .sql files
tsqlanalyze -i C:\scripts\Fabric.zip

## Analyze a live database
tsqlanalyze -c "Data Source=.\SQLEXPRESS;Initial Catalog=Chinook;Integrated
Security=True;Encrypt=false"
System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an 
object.
  at async Task<int> ErikEJ.SqlAnalyzer.Program.MainAsync(string[] args) in     
     Program.cs:76                                                              
  at async Task<int> ErikEJ.SqlAnalyzer.Program.Main(string[] args) in          
     Program.cs:45
